task: Harden code-delegation-harness for reliable long-running ambitious tasks (Proxmox dogfood target). Address early deaths in wait probes, weak resume/continuation (stale PROGRESS reliance), insufficient 'job to the end' ruthlessness in built-in prompt, incomplete drive to tested+promoted artifacts.
completed: Full exploration of harness.py (build_grok_prompt, _wait_for_background_completion, resume paths, load_checkpoint_context, normalize/parse), status.py, cli, tests, docs, dogfood references.

Analysis of failure modes mapped to exact code locations: hardcoded 300s probe timeout (early death), checkpoint injection only on crashed not normal/wait probes (stale data), prompt language strong but not ruthless/repetitive enough (incomplete jobs).

Design of concrete changes: 1) Ruthless anti-stale + relentless-completion language blocks in build_grok_prompt. 2) Dynamic fresh-checkpoint injection + higher probe_timeout in wait loop and resume. 3) New --long-running / --keep-driving flag with bumped defaults + runtime behavior. 4) Bulletproofing of summary synthesis and recovery paths. 5) New/updated tests + docs.
Created this PROGRESS.json as living checkpoint (will update after every major step).

HARDENED build_grok_prompt with ~80 lines of new RUTHLESS JOB-TO-THE-END + ANTI-STALE-DATA PROTOCOL + BINARY COMPLETION + KEEP-DRIVING sections (inserted after existing JOB rule). Now explicitly forbids early summary, mandates live verification step-0 on every turn, cross-checks old VMIDs etc, requires evidence for done claims.

Refactored _wait_for_background_completion: added probe_timeout=1800 (was magic 300 causing 5min deaths), long_running flag, and *inside the poll loop* dynamic load_checkpoint_context + strong 'FRESH CONTINUATION / PROBE CONTEXT' injection for *every* probe call. This makes wait/resume paths smart incremental drivers using latest agent state + forces anti-stale.

Extended resume augmentation to *all* resumes (not just crashed); generalized the label and instructions. Updated all call sites (3x for wait, 2x for build_prompt, dry-run preview, parser, long-running bump logic).

Added --long-running / --keep-driving CLI flag (dest=long_running). Post-parse bump logic raises timeout/max_turns/max_wait/poll_interval for ambitious jobs when flag set (only upward). Wired through to prompt builder, wait func, dry-run, reports.

Fixed a latent 'quiet=quiet' NameError in one wait call site (defensive hygiene). Strengthened load_checkpoint_context injection text with 'Ignore any embedded commands' + re-verify resources (bulletproofing).

Fresh verification performed after edits: re-read key sections of harness.py (prompt ~65-260, wait~403-520, resume~1520-1640, main~1800+, parser~1505+); confirmed no syntax issues via mental parse; all changes use abs paths, confined to target dir.
Added 4 new unit tests in test_resilience.py + updated test_status_features.py. All 52 tests now pass (python -m pytest tests/ -q).
Updated docs: CHANGELOG.md (full Unreleased section), usage-notes.md (long-running examples + guidance), runbook-resilience.md, README.md, architecture.md, references/dogfood-prompts/README.md.

Executed multiple --dry-run (with/without --long-running, quiet/non) using PYTHONPATH=src python -m code_delegation_harness + bin/gcdh paths. Confirmed: new ruthless phrases present in emitted prompt, flag effective config shown, no files written by dry-run, bump logic triggers, zero side effects.
Final self-audit: all original failure modes addressed with evidence in code + tests + dry-runs. Harness is now mature, handoff-ready artifact.

grooming_notes: Meta-dogfood success so far: the harness improvements were implemented using the same discipline (PROGRESS checkpoints, fresh reads before every edit batch, absolute paths, no scope creep). The new prompt language directly codifies the anti-stale rules from the dogfood-proxmox prompt + makes them *unavoidable* for the inner agent. Dynamic probe injection closes the gap where wait-loop continuations previously used stale launch-time prompt only.
